chainguard.capabilities.Capability.description:
Capability is an enumeration of the Chainguard IAM capabilities.

 - CAP_TENANT_VULN_REPORTS_LIST: TODO(colin): deprecate this cap once the legacy endpoint is removed
 - CAP_REPO_BLOBS_GET: repo.blobs.get is a capability required to pull container images.
We explicitly define this to distinguish roles that allow browsing/viewing
metadata associated with images vs pulling the image itself.
 - CAP_APK_BLOBS_GET: apk.blobs.get is a capability required to pull APKs.
We explicitly define this to distinguish roles that allow browsing/viewing
metadata associated with APKs vs pulling the APK itself.
 - CAP_BUILD_REPORT_LIST: Custom Image Build
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_ENTITLEMENTS_CREATE: Global libraries capabilities
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_PYTHON_LIST: Ecosystems specific capabilities
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_CACHE_INVALIDATE: Cache invalidation capability for the libraries cache (covers all ecosystems).
Granted to org owners and account admins; not to regular developer members.
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_REBUILDER_REQUESTS_CREATE: Rebuilder API capabilities
 - CAP_REGISTRY_ENTITLEMENTS_LIST: Registry Entitlements
 - CAP_REGISTRY_SETTINGS_LIST: Registry Settings
 - CAP_REGISTRY_DEPLOYMENTS_CREATE: Registry Deployments
 - CAP_POLICY_GATES_POLICY_CREATE: Policy Gates
 - CAP_TERMS_ACCEPT: Terms
 - CAP_GULFSTREAM: This is orthogonal enough that we should leave
it somewhat separate, so add new capabilities above.
TODO(mattmoor): Think about whether we can encode specific
controller capabilities into our access control here?
e.g. could each logical controller/webhook be its own
capability?
 - CAP_GUARDENER_DFC_CONVERT: Guardener
 - CAP_MCP_TOOL_CALL: MCP tool calls
 - CAP_SKILLS_PUBLISH: Skills registry — publishing skill artifacts to skills.cgr.dev
 - CAP_SKILLS_ENTITLEMENTS_CREATE: Skills registry entitlements

ecdhPublicKey.description:
When establishing a headless session, the client must first provide
their ECDH public key. The server will then generate an ephemeral
ECHD keypair and use the client's public key to establish a symmetric
key.

This symmetric key will be used to encrypt the idtoken and stored the
encrypted idtoken in the database for a short period, as well as
returning it to the client.

After the idtoken is encrypted, the server will discard both the shared
symmetric key and the ephemeral ECDH private key. The server ECDH will
be sent back to the client.

The client, with their private key, can use this public key to
establish the same symmetric key, which can be used to decrypt
the idtoken.

echd_public_key is the client's ECDH public key.

that service should assume.

app_installations maps app_id to the set of installations
for that app associated with this group.

installations.description: installations is the list of GitHub App installations.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.CheckEli.description: CheckEligibilityResponse is the response message for CheckEligibility.

eligible.description:
Indicates if the domain of the caller's email address is eligible
for email-domain-gated Groups flows.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Group.description: Group represents an IAM group (organization or folder).

description.description: Optional description of the group's purpose.

kind.description:
The organization kind. Required when creating a verified root group.
For subgroups, kind is inherited from the root group and cannot be set.

name.description:
Human-readable name of the group.
Must be 3-63 characters.

resourceLimits.description:
Resource limits indicating the maximum number of resources allowed
for this group by type.

status.description:
The provisioning status of the organization. Subgroups inherit the
status from the root group they are a descendant of.

uid.description:
Unique identifier for the group (UIDP).
Generated by server.

updateTime.description: When the group was last updated.

verified.description:
Whether this organization is verified by Chainguard.
Can only be set by Chainguard staff.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.GroupInv.description: GroupInvite represents a group invite code.

code.description: The invite code (only returned on create).

email.description:
Email address that is allowed to accept this invite code.
If blank, anyone with the invite code can accept.

keyId.description: The key ID for the invite verification key.

role.description:
The role associated with this invite, hydrated with name/description/capabilities.
Populated by the server on Get/List/Create responses.
May be absent if the referenced role has been deleted.

roleUid.description:
The role UIDP to assign when the invite is accepted.
Set this field when creating a group invite.

singleUse.description: Whether the invite can only be used once.

ttl.description:
Time-to-live duration for the invite. Required on create;
consumed to compute expiration_time and not returned in responses.

uid.description:
Unique identifier for the group invite (UIDP).
Generated by server.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.GroupInv.description: GroupInviteRole contains role details for a group invite.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.GroupsSe.description: RequestGroupAccessRequest is the request message for RequestGroupAccess.

company.description: Optional company associated with the identity.

email.description: Verified email address from OIDC token (email_verified = true).

emailUnverified.description: Unverified email address from OIDC token (email_verified = false).

lastSeenTime.description: When the identity was last federated (last login).

name.description:
Human-readable name of the identity.
Must be 3-63 characters.

providers.description: Cloud provider names associated with the identity.

uid.description:
Unique identifier for the identity (UIDP).

updateTime.description: When the identity was last updated.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Identity.description: AWSIdentity matches AWS IAM users and roles.

arnPattern.description: Regular expression for matching the ARN.

userIdPattern.description: Regular expression for matching the user ID.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Identity.description: ClaimMatch checks the third party IdP token's claims against configured patterns.

audiencePattern.description: Regular expression for matching the audience.

claimPatterns.description: Custom claims with regex pattern matching.

claims.description: Custom claims with exact value matching.

issuerPattern.description: Regular expression for matching the issuer.

subjectPattern.description: Regular expression for matching the subject.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Identity.description:
StaticKeys uses pre-registered verification keys for providers without an online issuer.
Note: Identities using static keys will expire (maximum 365 days).

audiences.description:
Accepted OIDC token audiences for this identity.
When set, only these values are checked and the global allowed
audiences are bypassed entirely.
When empty, falls back to the global allowed audiences.
Currently only a single audience is supported — callers must specify
exactly one value. The field is repeated for forward compatibility.

expirationTime.description:
When the issuer_keys expire.
Defaults to / Maximum of 365 days after creation.

issuer.description: Issuer to match against the `iss` claim.

subject.description: Subject to match against the `sub` claim.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Identity.description:
IdentityProvider represents the configuration parameters necessary for performing
authentication with an upstream identity provider.

createTime.description: When the identity provider was created.

defaultRole.description:
The UIDP of the default role to grant users of this identity provider
on initial authentication. Users who already have an identity on this platform
are unaffected.

description.description: Optional description of the identity provider.

name.description:
Human-readable name of the identity provider.
Must be 3-63 characters.

oidc.description: Defines the parameters necessary for authenticating with the OIDC flow.

uid.description:
Unique identifier for the identity provider (UIDP).
Generated by server.

updateTime.description: When the identity provider was last updated.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Identity.description: Configuration parameters for performing an OIDC token exchange.

additionalScopes.description:
Additional scopes to request for ID tokens from the upstream IdP.
Scope openid is always requested as part of the authentication flow.

clientId.description: Client ID of the identity provider. This is not considered sensitive.

clientSecret.description:
Client secret of the identity provider. This is a sensitive value
and is not returned for list/get calls.

group.description: The group matching the caller's email domain from their token.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.OrgKind.description:
OrgKind is the kind of organization the group belongs to.

 - ORG_KIND_UNSPECIFIED: Organization kind is not yet set.
 - ORG_KIND_STARTER: Free catalog starter organizations.
 - ORG_KIND_CUSTOMER: Paid customer organizations.
 - ORG_KIND_DEV: Personal developer organizations.
 - ORG_KIND_INFRA: Organizations that are used for infrastructure or automation.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.OrgStatu.description:
OrgStatus is the provisioning status of an organization.

 - ORG_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: Organization status is not yet set.
 - ORG_STATUS_INITIALIZING: The organization is awaiting provisioning setup by a reconciler.
 - ORG_STATUS_READY: The organization is fully provisioned and ready to use.
 - ORG_STATUS_SUSPENDED: The organization is suspended and unavailable to its members.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.RequestG.description:
RequestGroupAccessResponse is the response message for RequestGroupAccess.
It is intentionally empty; the access request is delivered out of band.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.Role.description: Role represents an IAM role with a set of capabilities.

capabilities.description:
Capabilities granted by this role.
Required on Create (at least one). Optional on Update (omit if not changing).

description.description: Optional description of the role's purpose.

uid.description:
Unique identifier for the role (UIDP).
Generated by server.

updateTime.description: When the role was last updated.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.RoleBind.description:
RoleBinding represents an IAM role binding.
A role binding associates an identity with a role within a group scope.

createTime.description: When the role binding was created.

group.description:
The group where this role binding applies.
Fully hydrated with group details.

identity.description:
The identity that is granted the role.
Fully hydrated with identity details.

identityUid.description:
The identity UID to bind to the role.
Set this field when creating or updating a role binding.

role.description:
The role that is granted to the identity.
Fully hydrated with role details.

roleUid.description:
The role UID to bind to the identity.
Set this field when creating or updating a role binding.

uid.description:
Unique identifier for the role binding (UIDP).
Generated by server.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.RoleBind.description:
RoleBindingGroup contains group details for a role binding.
This is a simplified view of Group for role binding display.

RoleBindingIdentity contains identity details for a role binding.
This is a simplified view of Identity for role binding display.

RoleBindingRole contains role details for a role binding.
This is a simplified view of Role for role binding display.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.ServiceP.description:
ServicePrincipal represents a Chainguard service that can assume an identity.

 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_UNSPECIFIED: Unknown/unspecified service principal.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_COSIGNED: Cosigned service for image signing.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_INGESTER: Ingester service for data ingestion.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_CATALOG_SYNCER: Catalog syncer service for synchronizing image catalogs.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_APKO_BUILDER: APKO builder service for building images.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_ENTITLEMENT_SYNCER: Entitlement syncer service for synchronizing entitlements.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_TENANT_SCANNER: Tenant scanner service for scanning tenant resources.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_SEDIMENTOLOGY: Sedimentology service for data analysis.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_SKILLUP: Skillup service for fixing repository skill violations.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_MATERIALIZER: Materializer service for materializing solutions from problem statements.

chainguard.platform.oidc.TokenPair.description:
ExchangeRefreshToken returns a pair of token, in order to allow
refresh token to also be rotated.

chainguard.platform.ping.v2beta1.PingRes.description: PingResponse is the response message for Ping.

chainguard.platform.registry.v2beta1.Cat.description:
CatalogTier represents the catalog tier classification for repositories.

 - CATALOG_TIER_UNSPECIFIED: Unknown or unspecified tier.
 - CATALOG_TIER_APPLICATION: Application images tier.
 - CATALOG_TIER_BASE: Base images tier.
 - CATALOG_TIER_FIPS: FIPS-compliant images tier.
 - CATALOG_TIER_AI: AI/ML images tier.
 - CATALOG_TIER_DEVTOOLS: Developer tools images tier.
 - CATALOG_TIER_COMMERCIAL: Commercial images tier.

packages.description: Packages to append to the image.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: Advisory is a security advisory the defines the relationship between a vulnerability and artifact.

advisoryId.title: advisory_id is the CGA identifier for the advisory

aliases.description: Other vulnerability identifiers this advisory applies to (CVE, GHSA, etc).

artifactName.title: The artifact name (e.g., wheel, jar name)

artifactType.title: The artifact type (e.g., apk, image, library)

author.description: The person or service account that created this advisory.

componentLocation.title: The filesystem location of the component

componentName.description: The name of the component within the subpackage where the vulnerability was matched.

deleteTime.description: When the advisory was deleted (if applicable).

events.description: Advisory events associated with this advisory (detected, fixed, etc).

legacyAdvisoryId.description: The advisory ID from the GitHub advisories repositories, if this advisory was migrated.

uid.description: The unique identifier (UIDP) for the Advisory.

updateTime.title: When the advisory was last updated

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: AdvisoryEvent represents a change in state during the lifecycle of an advisory.

analysisNotPlanned.description: Analysis of the vulnerability is not planned.

author.description: The person or service account who created this event.

createTime.description: The creation time of the event.

falsePositiveDetermination.description: The vulnerability is determined not to affect the artifact.

findings.description: The JSON-encoded struct containing vulnerability findings from a scanner.

fixNotPlanned.description: No fix is planned for this vulnerability.

fixed.description: The version at which a vulnerability is fixed.

patched.description: A patch has been applied to the given versions to address the vulnerability.

pendingUpstreamFix.description: A fix is pending on the upstream repository.

reviewState.description: The state of review for the event.

reviewer.description: The person who reviewed this event.

truePositiveDetermination.description: The vulnerability has been determined to actively affect the artifact.

uid.description: The unique identity (UIDP) of this event, a child of the advisory UIDP it belongs to.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: No analysis of the vulnerability is planned for the matched artifact.

note.description: Textual comments about why analysis is not planned for the vulnerability.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: Detection and acknowledgement of a vulnerability via scanner, NVD API, or manually.

nvdapi.description: The vulnerability was detected via the NVD API.

scanv1.description: The vulnerability was detected by a scanner.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: The vulnerability as detected via the NVD API.

cpeFound.description: CPE found that matches the artifact this vulnerability affects.

cpeSearched.description: The name or keyword used to determine the vulnerability match.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: The vulnerability was detected by a scanner, with the given match data.

component.description: The name of the component within the subpackage the vulnerability was detected din.

componentId.description: A scanner determined ID for the component the vulnerability was detected in.

componentLocation.description: The location within the subpackage of the matched component.

componentType.description: The type of the matched component.

componentVersion.description: The version of the matched component.

scanner.description: The scanner that detected the vulnerability.

subpackage.description: The subpackage the vulnerability was detected in. Matches the artifact name of the advisory.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description:
The vulnerability has been determined to not affect the matched artifact,
with reason and additional justification.

note.description: Additional textual comments about why this vulnerability was determined to be a false positive.

type.description: The type of false positive this vulnerability was determined to be.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description:
The type of false positive this vulnerability was determined to be.

 - TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: The unset false positive type. This value is typically not valid for interactions
with the API.
 - TYPE_VULNERABILITY_RECORD_ANALYSIS_CONTESTED: Indicates that the distro maintainers view the vulnerability record itself
to be describing a behavior that is not a security concern or that
misattributes security fault to the software in the distro package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_COMPONENT_VULNERABILITY_MISMATCH: Indicates that the component referred to by the vulnerability record is not
the component found in the distribution package. (For example, perhaps a
vulnerability scanner found a vulnerability for a package with the same name,
but for a different language ecosystem.)

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"component_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_VERSION_NOT_USED: Indicates that the vulnerability was correctly matched to the component, except
that the version(s) of the component referred to by the vulnerability record have
never been present in a release of the distribution package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PACKAGE: Indicates that the vulnerable code (e.g. a particular function) may have been
available for use or retrieved during the package build process but ultimately
was not included in the distro package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_NOT_IN_EXECUTION_PATH: Indicates that the vulnerable code (e.g. a particular function) is present in
the package, but it is impossible for this code to be executed in the package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_in_execute_path" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_CANNOT_BE_CONTROLLED_BY_ADVERSARY: Indicates that the vulnerable code is present and able to be executed, but
not in a way that can be exploited by an adversary.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_cannot_be_controlled_by_adversary" justification.
 - TYPE_INLINE_MITIGATIONS_EXIST: Indicates that the vulnerable code is present and able to be exploited by an
adversary, but that the vulnerability is mitigated by other code in the package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"inline_mitigations_already_exist" justification.
A fix is not planned for the vulnerability in the matched artifact.

note.description: Textual comments about why a fix is not planned for the vulnerability.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: The vulnerability has been fixed at the given version.

fixedVersion.description: The first version of the artifact in which the vulnerability is considered fixed.

note.description: Additional textual comments about the fix.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: The vulnerability has been patched at the given versions of the artifact.

note.description: Textual comments about the patch applied to fix the vulnerability.

patchedVersions.description: The versions of the artifact that were patched to address the vulnerability.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: A fix is pending on the upstream repository.

note.description: Textual comments about the upstream fix.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description: The vulnerability has been determined to affect the matched artifact.

note.description: Textual comments about why this vulnerability was determined to be a true positive.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description:
- REVIEW_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unknown/unspecified review status. This is not a valid status for an event.
 - REVIEW_STATE_PENDING: The event is pending review.
 - REVIEW_STATE_APPROVED: The event has been approved. This is a terminal state.
 - REVIEW_STATE_REQUEST_CHANGES: Changes have been requested by a reviewer.
 - REVIEW_STATE_REJECTED: The event has been rejected. This is a terminal state.

get.description: ListSubscriptions returns subscriptions matching filter criteria.

parameters.description: ancestors_of are groups reachable by repeated proceeding from child to parent.
parameters.name: uidp.ancestorsOf

parameters.description: descendants_of are groups reachable by repeated proceeding from parent to child.
parameters.name: uidp.descendantsOf

parameters.description: children_of are groups reachable by directly proceeding from parent to children.
parameters.name: uidp.childrenOf

parameters.description: in_root resticts responses to root level resources (organizations, user identities)
parameters.name: uidp.inRoot

parameters.description: ids are a list of exact UIDPs of records.
parameters.name: uidp.ids

parameters.description:
Maximum number of results to return per page.
Default: 50, Maximum: 200.
parameters.name: pageSize

parameters.description:
Page token from a previous List response for pagination.
Opaque token with 3-day expiration.
parameters.name: pageToken

parameters.description:
Order results by field. Format: "field [asc|desc]"
Default: ascending by primary key.
Note: Changing order_by between pages invalidates the page token.
parameters.name: orderBy

parameters.description:
Number of results to skip before returning.
Used for random-access pagination (jumping to arbitrary pages).

patch.description:
UpdateGroup updates the given Group according to the provided field mask.
The fully populated Group is returned.

parameters.description:
Unique identifier for the group (UIDP).
Generated by server.
parameters.name: group.uid

description.description: Optional description of the group's purpose.

kind.description:
The organization kind. Required when creating a verified root group.
For subgroups, kind is inherited from the root group and cannot be set.

name.description:
Human-readable name of the group.
Must be 3-63 characters.

resourceLimits.description:
Resource limits indicating the maximum number of resources allowed
for this group by type.

status.description:
The provisioning status of the organization. Subgroups inherit the
status from the root group they are a descendant of.

updateTime.description: When the group was last updated.

verified.description:
Whether this organization is verified by Chainguard.
Can only be set by Chainguard staff.

post.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND when the supplied group_uid does not resolve to the
verified root group matching the caller's email domain — either because
no such group exists, the group_uid is wrong, or the caller is not on a
trusted upstream identity provider. Like LookupGroup, these cases are
intentionally collapsed.

Returns RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (HTTP 429) when the same caller has recently
requested access to the same group; callers should surface a "request
already sent" message rather than retrying.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT when group_uid is empty.

parameters.name: groupUid

post.description: CreateGroup creates a new group (organization or folder).

parameters.description:
Parent group UIDP. Empty for creating root organizations.
Can be a UIDP, suffix, or name of a group in scope.
parameters.name: parent

requestBody.description: The group to create. Required fields: name.

delete.description:
DeleteGroup deletes a group by UID.
Fails if the group has child repos, child groups, or other child IAM resources.

parameters.description: UID of the group to delete.
parameters.name: uid

get.description: GetGroup retrieves a single group by UID.

parameters.description: UID of the group to retrieve.
parameters.name: uid

get.description:
CheckEligibility reports whether the caller passes the same eligibility
gates applied by LookupGroup and RequestGroupAccess. A response of
eligible=true does not guarantee those calls succeed for other reasons
(no matching org, already requested, etc.).

Returns NOT_FOUND when:
  * no verified root group matches the caller's email domain;
  * the caller's token is not from a trusted upstream identity provider; or
  * the caller's email domain is on the blocked-domains list (free email,
    known competitor, etc.).

These cases are intentionally collapsed into a single response so callers
cannot distinguish "no such org" from "you're not allowed to know."

parameters.description: Deprecated: use scopes instead
parameters.name: scope

parameters.description: List of capabilities to request for the token.
parameters.name: cap

parameters.description: Empty or the UIDP of the custom identity provider.
parameters.name: identityProvider

parameters.description:
One or more group scopes to restrict the returned token to.
If scope and scopes are both provided, the union of their values is
considered, after deduplication.

The returned token will include role bindings for the requested scopes,
if any exist, that are either granted directly at that scope or inherited from
its ancestors. That is, a role binding granted at a "lower" scope in the ancestry
applies to all descendants of that scope.

For example, given a role binding on group `foo` with id `foo/rb-id-1`
and a role binding on group `foo/bar` with id `foo/bar/rb-id-2`:
 - given scopes = [foo, foo/bar] => {foo: [foo/rb-id-1], foo/bar: [foo/bar/rb-id-2]}
 - given scopes = [foo/bar] => {foo/bar: [foo/rb-id-1, foo/bar/rb-id-2]}
parameters.name: scopes

parameters.name: scope

its ancestors. That is, a role binding granted at a "lower" scope in the ancestry
applies to all descendants of that scope.

For example, given a role binding on group `foo` with id `foo/rb-id-1`
and a role binding on group `foo/bar` with id `foo/bar/rb-id-2`:
 - given scopes = [foo, foo/bar] => {foo: [foo/rb-id-1], foo/bar: [foo/bar/rb-id-2]}
 - given scopes = [foo/bar] => {foo/bar: [foo/rb-id-1, foo/bar/rb-id-2]}

parameters.description:
List advisories that match the list of event review states (e.g., PENDING, REQUEST_CHANGES).
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event with any of the provided
statuses will be returned. This is useful for review workflows where users need to see
only events requiring action.

 - REVIEW_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unknown/unspecified review status. This is not a valid status for an event.
 - REVIEW_STATE_PENDING: The event is pending review.
 - REVIEW_STATE_APPROVED: The event has been approved. This is a terminal state.
 - REVIEW_STATE_REQUEST_CHANGES: Changes have been requested by a reviewer.
 - REVIEW_STATE_REJECTED: The event has been rejected. This is a terminal state.
parameters.name: eventReviewStates

parameters.description:
Filters by event author email
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event created by the specified
author will be returned. This enables users to find their own events or events from
specific team members.
parameters.name: eventAuthors

parameters.description:
exclude_event_author filters out advisories where any event was created by the specified author
When specified, advisories containing events by this author will be excluded from results.
This is useful for review workflows where users want to see all pending events except their own,
enabling them to review others' work without seeing their own submissions.
parameters.name: excludeEventAuthors

parameters.description:
event_reviewer filters by event reviewer email
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event reviewed by the specified
author will be returned. This enables users to find their own events or events from
specific team members.
parameters.name: eventReviewers

parameters.description:
event_types filters by event type
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event of any of the provided
types will be returned.

 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_UNSPECIFIED: Default/unspecified
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_DETECTION: Detection event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_TRUE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: True positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIXED: Fixed event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FALSE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: False positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_ANALYSIS_NOT_PLANNED: Analysis not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIX_NOT_PLANNED: Fix not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PENDING_UPSTREAM_FIX: Pending upstream fix
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PATCHED: Patched event
parameters.name: eventTypes

parameters.description:
latest_event_type filters out advisories where the _most recent approved event_ is
of the given type. This is useful to find advisories which are currently detected,
fixed, patched, etc.

 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_UNSPECIFIED: Default/unspecified
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_DETECTION: Detection event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_TRUE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: True positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIXED: Fixed event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FALSE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: False positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_ANALYSIS_NOT_PLANNED: Analysis not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIX_NOT_PLANNED: Fix not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PENDING_UPSTREAM_FIX: Pending upstream fix
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PATCHED: Patched event
parameters.name: latestEventType

parameters.description:
Maximum number of results to return per page.
Default: 50, Maximum: 200.
parameters.name: pageSize

parameters.description:
Page token from a previous List response for pagination.
Opaque token with 3-day expiration.
parameters.name: pageToken

parameters.description:
Order results by field. Format: "field [asc|desc]"
Default: "uid asc"
Note: Changing order_by between pages invalidates the page token.
parameters.name: orderBy

parameters.description:
Number of results to skip before returning.
Used for random-access pagination (jumping to arbitrary pages).
Can be combined with page_token to skip from cursor position.
Must be non-negative.
parameters.name: skip

parameters.description:
Free-text search query. Performs case-insensitive substring matching across
artifact_name, component_name, and aliases. Results match if any of these
fields contain the query string. Can be combined with other filters.
parameters.name: query

get.description: GetAdvisory retrieves a single advisory by UID.

parameters.description: UID of the advisory to retrieve.
parameters.name: uid
